data.description:
## testing_mode: GREEN

Add a CI lint step (and a corresponding test) that asserts every check-context name in .github/required-checks.txt corresponds to an actual job that emits that context in a GitHub Actions workflow file. This prevents silent name drift when workflow jobs are renamed.

## Approach

### Part 1: CI lint script at plugins/dso/scripts/onboarding/validate-required-checks.sh

Usage: validate-required-checks.sh [--checks-file <path>] [--workflows-dir <path>]

Logic:
1. Read all non-comment lines from checks-file (default: .github/required-checks.txt)
2. For each check name, search all workflow YAML files in workflows-dir (default: .github/workflows/) for a job 'name:' field value that matches (case-sensitive, exact string)
3. GitHub matrix job names use template expansion: 'Ticket tests (${{ matrix.name }})' → matches 'Ticket tests (linux-bash4)'. The validator should understand this pattern: if a job name field contains '${{ matrix.' it should expand known matrix values and check each.
4. Report any check name that has NO matching job 'name:' in any workflow file
5. Exit non-zero if any unmatched names are found

Uses python3 (already available in CI) for YAML parsing.

### Part 2: Add validate-required-checks step to ci.yml

In the existing CI workflow (.github/workflows/ci.yml), add a new job:

  validate-required-checks:
    name: Validate required-checks.txt
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 5
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - name: Validate check-context names
        run: python3 plugins/dso/scripts/onboarding/validate-required-checks.sh

Note: ci.yml is owned by story d025-87d4. Coordinate the exact YAML edit — do NOT duplicate job names that d025-87d4 will add. If d025-87d4 has not yet modified ci.yml at the time this task executes, add the validate-required-checks job and note in the commit that it coordinates with d025-87d4.

### Part 3: Test at tests/scripts/test-validate-required-checks.sh

Tests:
1. test_script_exists — validate-required-checks.sh exists and is executable
2. test_all_names_match_exits_zero — With mock workflows dir where all names resolve, exits 0
3. test_unmatched_name_exits_nonzero — With a mock checks file containing a name not in any workflow, exits non-zero and reports the name
4. test_matrix_expansion — A checks file with 'Ticket tests (linux-bash4)' matches a workflow job named 'Ticket tests (${{ matrix.name }})' when matrix.name can be linux-bash4
5. test_ignores_comment_lines — Lines starting with # are not validated

## Done Definition
- validate-required-checks.sh is executable, passes ShellCheck
- test-validate-required-checks.sh has 5 tests, all GREEN
- ci.yml contains a validate-required-checks job that runs the script
- Running validate-required-checks.sh against the repo with the current .github/required-checks.txt exits 0 (all names resolve)
data.title: GREEN: Add CI lint step asserting required-checks.txt names match actual workflow job contexts
